key_points: Off-campus landlords MPM and Chapter have serious maintenance and scam complaints
Chapter apartments had floods, broken locks, one-way staircases, egregious move-out charges
Gas leak in Gorham apartment went undetected by fire department
key_quotes: A maintenance log should NOT have a scroll bar, and security issues like the front door locks are insane
Move out charges were egregious (like $400 for a small dent in a kitchen appliance). Don't believe any 5 star review
NEVER RENT WITH MPM!!! they will prey on students x10, never maintain their properties, and the lease has hella loopholes

key_points: Large school means real risk of social isolation, especially for introverts
Party culture is strong but students litter lakes and embarrass the school
Football student tickets nearly impossible to get; scalpers charge $1000+
key_quotes: It's 2 months into 2nd semester of my freshman year, and I honestly have never felt more isolated, depressed and suicidal
They need to actually ban people who scalp. You shouldn't see dozens of scalpers minutes after the sale selling for $1000
I've been here 4 years. Assume every guy you interact with hasn't washed his hands

key_points: Rathskeller removed tater tots without explanation, frustrating regulars
Rheta's Market ran out of cheese curds on National Cheese Curd Day
Price disparities across campus dining locations are notable and annoying
key_quotes: How do you run out of cheese curds in Wisconsin
Union south is cheaper — the view alone is worth $0.70
If you come to terms with the fact that everything in college is a scam, shit like this ceases to surprise you

key_points: Students openly discuss depression, isolation, and suicidal ideation on campus forums
UHS mental health services are recommended and reportedly helpful
Campus community responds with warmth; peer support notes found in buildings
key_quotes: I honestly have never felt more isolated, depressed and suicidal since I've been here
You need to get in touch with UHS mental health. They're really good and they can help you develop a plan
Pulling all-nighters shouldn't be the college aesthetic — lack of sleep literally makes it harder to learn

key_points: OOS tuition runs ~$58k/year with little merit aid expected
Even UW students advise OOS students to reconsider the cost
Tuition hike announced late summer 2025, blamed partly on federal funding cuts
key_quotes: OOS students paying 60k/year and you can't afford a fucking email address for alumni
No, we can't convince you — either it's worth the cost for your family or it's not
Thanks Trump — cuts to federal funding have to be made up somehow

key_points: Stats department called a 'complete dumpster fire'; AI-generated exam questions reported
Cheating is a known widespread problem; professors say university discourages enforcement
Research reputation is elite — ranked 4th nationally for producing faculty, 18th for Nobel laureates
key_quotes: Stats department continues to be a complete dumpster fire
The university has set incentives to prevent us from being effective against [cheating]. Actually addressing it would mean admitting they have a problem
It's a research first school and it's good at it, even though sometimes that focus means profs are great at research but not so great at teaching

key_points: Three chancellors in quick succession; current one just left for Columbia after 3 years
DEI division dissolved and diversity website dismantled under political pressure
Chancellor called in police on campus protesters, earning widespread student criticism
key_quotes: She's making over a million a year... She made it three years. Get ready for another chancellor search
Enough with the arrogant people using UW as just another step in their career
Probably a good move given the dysfunction of that division, but what they aren't saying is this was part of the deal they cut with the GOP to get buildings funded

key_points: Madison is widely loved — city consistently praised even by critical students
Bascom Hill is brutal, especially in winter; calves get a workout
Campus has stunning lake views, auroras, and genuinely beautiful spaces
key_quotes: Nobody told me how goated of a town Madison was
By graduation your calves will look like that of a Greek god's
Some cities are in amazing cities that make up for the underwhelming campus — like Madison

key_points: CS ranked ~#13 nationally despite 45% overall acceptance rate
Strong research pipeline: 4th in producing faculty, 26 Nobel laureates affiliated
Wisconsin School of Business is a direct-admit program with solid regional reputation
key_quotes: UW-Madison ranked fourth nationally in producing faculties
Just got accepted direct to UW Madison Wisconsin School of Business. Officially logging off this sub forever
It's a research first school — grads get a lot of opportunity to teach and other colleges don't have that

key_points: Exceptional value for in-state students; poor value for OOS at $58k+
UW community itself tells OOS admits to reconsider and go in-state instead
Food pantry on campus signals real financial stress among student population
key_quotes: Probably better you convince her to go in-state and take on as little debt as possible
I've used Open Seat [food pantry] when times got rough... things are looking rough again
I AM paying 30k a year to go here, so I do want to get the most out of it

key_points: Strong school spirit, beloved traditions, and a city students genuinely love
Big school loneliness is real — social life requires active effort to build
Quirky, self-aware community that jokes about itself but clearly cares about UW
key_quotes: What if instead of Wisconsin it was Wisprosin and instead of the Badgers we were the Goodgers
See how these midwesterners are all about that pragmatic, reasonable approach? That's one reason to send her
I lowk even felt inspired by awkwardly singing Varsity with kids I never met before, shi was beautiful

key_points: **Academic Integrity:** Cheating is rampant and professors say the university actively suppresses enforcement
**Housing:** Multiple off-campus landlords (MPM, Chapter) have documented patterns of predatory practices toward students
**Safety:** Dorm bathroom voyeurism arrest involved 40-50 videos; laser pointer harassment reported at off-campus housing
key_quotes: It's not just the Indians. It's a big university-wide problem, because everyone knows that UW doesn't actually care to do anything about it
UW student arrested for recording others in a dorm bathroom has now been accused of taking up to 50 videos
They will gaslight you about every service request, never maintain their properties, and the lease has hella loopholes

key_points: URS (Undergraduate Research Scholars) program is underutilized but highly recommended
Morgridge Hall's new CS building has a stunning spiral staircase and impressive design
Campus has plasma fusion research, 900MHz NMR machines, and serious science infrastructure
key_quotes: I already got URS as one of them — any advice on other hidden gem opportunities?
One of my classes is on the fourth floor and staring out makes me feel like Mufasa looking out at all the light touches
It's a giant hollow metal sphere that magnetically contains plasma — there's actually two more experiments like this at UW
